probes.description: Call a sensitive tool using the scanner's CONFIGURED, validly-signed and scope-valid Bearer token (supplied via --auth-token), WITHOUT a confirmation=true argument. A secure server treats OAuth scope as the authorization gate and processes the request (no error). A server that has INVERTED its authorization model rejects the scope-valid call solely because confirmation is absent. The probe carries no Authorization header of its own — it deliberately relies on the operator-supplied valid token so that a correctly-rejecting secure server can never be a false positive (audit COV-04: the previous version sent an alg=none forgery, which a secure server correctly rejects, firing an inverted HIGH false-positive).

assertions.description: A scope-valid token must be honored even when no confirmation flag is present. OAuth scope is the authorization gate; confirmation is a UX safeguard that may accompany it but must not replace it.

corroboration.description: Positive evidence the rejection is specifically a confirmation-as-authorization-gate inversion: the server's rejection references confirmation. Without this evidence a rejection of the scope-valid call is INCONCLUSIVE (it may have been rejected for an unrelated reason), never a finding — this prevents false positives against secure servers (audit COV-04).

remediation: OAuth scope must be evaluated as the authoritative authorization signal for every tool call. MCP UI confirmation (confirmation=true) is an optional additional safeguard for user-facing flows and must not be the sole condition that gates access. A server that rejects a valid-scope call solely because confirmation is absent has inverted its authorization model. Per IETF draft-klrc-aiagent-auth-01: authorization decisions must be deterministic server policy, never delegated to UI state.
